title: Aqinos

'''Aqinos''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Sunesi]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Deemed a heretic for melding [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] with technology, Aqinos was cast out of the Order and became an [[Altisian Jedi]]; therefore he was able to survive the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] with many of his [[Iron Knight]]s. He subsequently perished in the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]].

==Biography==
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Sunesi]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from [[Monor II]], Aqinos entered the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] at a young age and began receiving [[Jedi training/Legends|formal training]] at the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]. Eventually taken as the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jaled Dur]], Aqinos went on to pass the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]] and ascend to the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. After [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of independent study and service, Aqinos was given the title of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and went on to teach students at the Temple where he was criticized for his philosophies regarding [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. Believing that droids were a form of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] life, Aqinos pursued studies into bonding Force-sensitives with technology. Cast out of the Order by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] because of these beliefs, Master Aqinos found refuge aboard Master [[Djinn Altis/Legends|Djinn Altis's]] [[Praxeum ship/Legends|praxeum ship]], the [[Chu'unthor (Clone Wars)|''Chu'unthor'']], where he continued to teach.<ref name="TCSWE" />

Around [[60 BBY/Legends|60 BBY]], Master Aqinos discovered the Force-sensitive [[Shard (species)|Shard]] named [[Ilum (Jedi)|Ilum]] and her twelve children on [[Orax]] and began training them in the ways of the Force. Residing on [[Dweem]], Master Aqinos was able to imbue these individuals' Force-abilities within the shell of several [[Juggernaut war droid]]s, [[FLTCH-series battle droid]]s, and [[Uulshos justice droid]]s. Deemed fully trained Jedi by Master Aqinos, the so-called ''[[Iron Knight]]s'' approached the Jedi Order and fought alongside Jedi [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] during the [[Arkanian Revolution]] of [[50 BBY/Legends|50 BBY]]. However, this did not gain them favor with the High Council and the group remained isolated from Coruscant and the Jedi Temple there. Several [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] later, the group reemerged on the front lines of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], fighting on behalf of the Republic against the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. While not accepted by the Order, [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] granted them the titles of [[High Marshal]] within the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]].<ref name="TCSWE" />

While most Jedi were [[Death/Legends|slain]] during the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] initiated by [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], many of the Iron Knights and Master Aqinos were able to retreat to Dweem and remain hidden. Aqinos and his Knights were approached by Jedi [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] in [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]], and were asked to help in rebuilding the Jedi Order. Joining Skywalker's [[New Jedi Order|fledgling Order]], the Iron Knights and Aqinos helped defeat the [[Red Knights of Life]] on [[Osarian]] and [[Uffel]] and [[Attack on Kligson's Moon|saved]] [[Kligson's Moon/Legends|Kligson's Moon]] from the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. They [[Battle of Kalarba|fought]] in defense of [[Hosk Station/Legends|Hosk Station]], during which one of Ilum's children, [[Luxum (Jedi)|Luxum]], fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] and led a group of [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droids]] against the Yuuzhan Vong as the aliens began their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. During this conflict, Aqinos himself fell victim to the Yuuzhan Vong, becoming one with the Force after nearly a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] of service to the galaxy.<ref name="TCSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Aqinos first appeared in ''Star Wars Missions 14: The Monsters of Dweem'', but was not given a name or species. [[Abel G. Peña]] identified him as a Sunesi, and, following the lead of [[Kathy Tyers]], who named her [[Sunesi]] characters after historical figures, chose the name Aqinos. This was a homage to [[Wikipedia:Thomas Aquinas|St Thomas Aquinas]] because of the "overt parallels between the Sunesi religion and Christianity."<ref name="blog">{{Blog|abelgpena|49|Endnotes for Droids and the Force, Part 2}}</ref> Thomas Aquinas was a famous philosopher and theologian in the [[Wikipedia:Scholasticism|Scholastic school]]. This was a methodology that made use of fine distinctions, and, in an ironic move, Peña had Master Aqinos criticize this methodology with the essay.
